steps.title: Set up the substitution test
steps.content: A point is a solution to $y \lt -4x + 4$ if substituting its coordinates makes the inequality true. We'll test each answer choice.

steps.title: Backsolving
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B $(0, 5)$: Is $5 \lt -4(0) + 4 = 4$? No, $5 > 4$ fails.

Testing choice A $(-4, 0)$: Is $0 \lt -4(-4) + 4 = 16 + 4 = 20$? Yes, $0 \lt 20$ works. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Check the other choices
steps.content: Choice B fails because $5$ is not less than $4$. Choice C fails because $1$ is not less than $-4$. Choice D also fails because $-1$ is not less than $-4$. Only choice A satisfies the inequality.
